name: Running circular around Monte Arabi and Cueva Horadada
name_original: Running circular por el Monte Arabí y la Cueva Horadada
region_intro: Jogging around Yecla offers a diverse range of natural landscapes, from mountains and plains to ravines and unique geological formations. The region is characterized by significant natural monuments like Monte Arabí, known for its striking rock formations and archaeological value. Runners can explore varied terrain, including the impressive Sierra Salinas massif with its succession of hills and deep ravines, and the flatter Vía Verde del Chicharra. These features provide a mix of challenging and accessible running routes. Best jogging routes around Yecla The most popular jogging route is Running circular around Monte Arabi and Cueva Horadada, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete, exploring the unique geomorphology of Monte Arabí. Another top favourite among local runners is Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí – Monte Arabí loop from Monumento Natural Monte Arabí, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.7 km) path. This route winds through the natural monument, offering views of ancient rock art and distinctive rock formations. Local runners also love the Cueva Cálida loop from Colonias Sierra Salinas Yecla, a 13.8 miles (22.3 km) trail leading through the Sierra Salinas massif, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Jogging around Yecla is defined by mountains, ravines, and unique geological formations, with options ranging from flat greenways to challenging mountain trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy runs to difficult ascents. The routes in Yecla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 9 reviews. More than 380 runners have used komoot to explore Yecla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Yecla?
A: Yecla offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 50 trails to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging ascents through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Yecla?
A: Jogging routes in Yecla feature incredibly diverse terrain. You'll find everything from the flat, accessible paths of the &lt;em&gt;Vía Verde del Chicharra&lt;/em&gt;, perfect for a relaxed run, to the rugged mountains and deep ravines of &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Salinas&lt;/strong&gt; and the unique rock formations of &lt;strong&gt;Monte Arabí&lt;/strong&gt;. This variety ensures options for all preferences, from gentle jogs through vineyards to demanding mountain runs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Yecla?
A: Yes, for families or those seeking an easier run, the &lt;em&gt;Vía Verde del Chicharra&lt;/em&gt; is an excellent choice. This greenway, built on an old railway line, offers a flat and accessible path, particularly suitable for families and beginners. It winds through an agrarian landscape with extensive vineyards, providing a pleasant and safe environment.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Yecla?
A: Absolutely. Many of Yecla's running trails are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900943488/running-circular-around-monte-arabi-and-cueva-horadada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular around Monte Arabi and Cueva Horadada&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the unique geomorphology of Monte Arabí. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20131687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí – Monte Arabí loop from Monumento Natural Monte Arabí&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of ancient rock art.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while running in Yecla?
A: Yecla's routes are rich with natural and archaeological wonders. You can explore the striking rock formations and prehistoric rock art at &lt;strong&gt;Monte Arabí&lt;/strong&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí&lt;/a&gt; is a significant highlight. In &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Salinas&lt;/strong&gt;, you'll encounter impressive mountainous massifs, deep ravines, and dense Aleppo-pine woodlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4264671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Lagrimal&lt;/a&gt; is another interesting cave to discover.

Q: Are there challenging running trails for experienced runners in Yecla?
A: Yes, experienced runners will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Salinas&lt;/strong&gt; massif, with its succession of hills and deep ravines, offers demanding terrain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Cálida loop from Colonias Sierra Salinas Yecla&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.3 km (13.8 miles) trail with significant elevation gain, providing a rigorous workout amidst wild landscapes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Yecla?
A: The running routes in Yecla are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 3.9 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 380 runners have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the diverse terrain, from the unique geomorphology of Monte Arabí to the tranquil woodlands of Sierra Salinas.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer scenic viewpoints in Yecla?
A: Many routes in Yecla provide stunning panoramic views. Trails around &lt;strong&gt;Monte Arabí&lt;/strong&gt; offer fabulous vistas of the surrounding countryside, especially from higher points. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Salinas&lt;/strong&gt; massif also features elevated sections where you can enjoy expansive views over the Altiplano region. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabasa Viewpoint – Sierra de Salinas loop from Colonias Sierra Salinas Yecla&lt;/a&gt; for a route specifically highlighting scenic spots.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Yecla?
A: Yecla's climate makes it suitable for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in winter, the weather can be mild enough for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended during this season to avoid the peak heat.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Yecla?
A: Yes, Yecla has options for beginners. The &lt;em&gt;Vía Verde del Chicharra&lt;/em&gt; is particularly well-suited for easy runs due to its flat and well-maintained surface. It's a great starting point for those new to jogging or looking for a less strenuous experience, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without significant elevation changes.

Q: How long are the running routes around Yecla generally?
A: The running routes around Yecla vary significantly in length. You can find shorter trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900943488/running-circular-around-monte-arabi-and-cueva-horadada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular around Monte Arabi and Cueva Horadada&lt;/a&gt; at about 7.3 km (4.5 miles), which takes around an hour. For longer distances, routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Cálida loop from Colonias Sierra Salinas Yecla&lt;/a&gt; extend to over 22 km (13.8 miles), requiring several hours to complete.
tips: An impressive cave that is a perfect shelter to rest during the hike.
Monte Arabí is an interesting mountain range that stands out in the Altiplano region (Murcia), about 17 kilometers northwest of Yecla.
